title: Just made an AI trading system that is kinda profitable
author: No-Arm-2779
selftext:
I was kind of messing around with ideas and got something that beat sp500 two days in a row with a paper money account and locally hosted AI models. Made about 2% return over 2 days.

[https://github.com/Matthew-Doe/AI\_trading](https://github.com/Matthew-Doe/AI_trading)

comments.author: reggievicktwo
comments.body:
Nice start. Two days isn’t meaningful yet, but beating the S&P short-term is a good signal to keep testing.

If you want a cleaner way to track performance and avoid bias, check out https://AlphaLens.dev. Makes it easier to monitor returns, consistency, and compare against benchmarks over time.

replies.author: No-Arm-2779
replies.body: Thanks! Do you have any recommendations for how long I should wait and observe before giving it a little bit of real cash?

replies.author: dwajxd
replies.body: I don’t know a lot about ai trading but if I would start giving it real capital, I would first backtest it over 5-7 years time frame, also test it with synergic data, if still successful, paper trade for 2-3 months and only then add 30% of planned capital and slowly scale it up

comments.author: Late-Sun-3805
comments.body: Working on the same. Thanks for the encouragement. Your abit ahead of me.

replies.author: No-Arm-2779
replies.body: You're welcome! I built it using codex if you want to try accelerating your development.

comments.author: klymaxx45
comments.body: How much of your capital did you use to make 2%?

replies.author: No-Arm-2779
replies.body: For the initial run it used basically all so $100k. Mostly a good bet on Oracle that it miraculously sold right before it dipped and then bought the dip. Afterwards I made rules on using more than a certain percentage of the remaining cash.

replies.author: klymaxx45
replies.body: Nice! ORCL was good. Caught that one for 244% currently and it peaked at 435%.

comments.author: IndyJoeDv
comments.body: keep in mind going from paper to real time you're going to encounter slippage in terms of fills. normally won't be as dramatic as backtest fills, but expect returns to drop in real time. And make sure to count for commissions and fees, which you may already be doing, just a word of advice to others. otherwise a somewhat profitable strategy can go negative very very quickly.

replies.author: No-Arm-2779
replies.body: All you need is an alpaca API key and some ollama models. Honestly the easiest way to set it up is to download codex, give it the link to the repo and tell it to set it up

replies.author: Bixby_3
replies.body: Okay, thank you very much. Does it work on xauusd??

replies.author: No-Arm-2779
replies.body: I'm not sure

comments.author: el_blanco_antrax
comments.body: wow amazing !! hot did you do it ? ( I'm trying with Claude Ai but I'm a beginner :(

replies.author: No-Arm-2779
replies.body: I built it all with the openai equivalent to Claude code. Feel free to use mine. Link to the GitHub in the post

replies.author: el_blanco_antrax
replies.body: Thank you so much !! I'll download it now ... very nice job !!! :)

comments.author: PassiveBotAI
comments.body:
Kinda profitable after 2 days is exactly the right way to frame it. Two days is not a track record, it is a promising start, and the fact that you know that already puts you ahead of most people who post their first two green days like they cracked the code.

The locally hosted models angle is interesting — what are you using for inference and how are you handling the latency between signal generation and order execution? That gap is where a lot of AI trading systems lose edge that looks clean in backtests.

2% over 2 days on paper is a good signal to keep going. The real test is what happens over 30 trades across different market conditions. Same setup in a trending market versus a choppy one versus a news-driven session will tell you a lot more about whether the edge is real or regime-dependent.

What does your signal pipeline look like — are the models doing price prediction, sentiment analysis, or something else entirely?

replies.author: No-Arm-2779
replies.body: I'm not so worried about latency because I'm not building it to do day trading but rather to simply buy low and hold until the AI models think that they might start going down. I chose local inference since I've got a lot of vram on my PC and I've been really interested in local inferences. If I ever do put a little bit of cash in I may switch to a cloud model like Gemini or Claude. The signal pipeline is I have a AI model search across 200 symbols for 20 that might make a move, I then have 2 AI models for each symbol, one make 5-7 reasons why it might act bearish and the only bullish and then a larger model evaluates the arguments and then makes a decision and executes it if it's confidence is greater than 0.6. the whole thing takes like 15 minutes

replies.author: PassiveBotAI
replies.body:
The adversarial debate structure is clever — bear case versus bull case evaluated by a larger model. That naturally forces the system to surface disconfirming evidence rather than just confirming the initial signal, which is where most single-model systems fail.

The 15-minute execution window is fine for swing holds. The risk is if the screening model flags a move that is already 80 percent complete by the time the debate resolves. Worth tracking entry timing relative to the initial signal — if you are consistently late the pipeline needs a fast-path for high-conviction setups.

What confidence threshold did you land on 0.6 and how sensitive are results to that number? 🎯

replies.author: No-Arm-2779
replies.body: It's only been 2 days so I don't have enough data. 0.6 felt like a decent starting point. I'll definitely be updating the threshold as needed

replies.author: PassiveBotAI
replies.body: Two days is honest — 0.6 is probably fine as a starting point but the number that matters more is how it behaves at 0.61 vs 0.59. If results cliff-edge at the threshold you've found a real signal. If they degrade gradually it's noise. Worth running a sensitivity sweep across 0.5-0.7 before committing to it. 🎯

comments.author: Forsaken_Leader_8
comments.body: Making 2 percent in two days on a local model is a solid start for paper trading. If you are looking to scale this or add more features to your system, you should check out the data sets on signalwhisper.com. They have clean feeds for congressional trades and dark pool activity that work really well as additional inputs for AI models. Adding that kind of institutional flow data can usually help reduce the false positives that local LLMs sometimes run into with just price action.

comments.author: FantasticShine4012
comments.body: If you don’t mind i wil backtest this on 25 years data m1,m5,m15,m30,h1 and d1 on all markets.  I will give you the output

replies.author: No-Arm-2779
replies.body: Wow really! That's awesome thank you so much!

replies.author: FantasticShine4012
replies.body: 10 hours and not finished yet

replies.author: No-Arm-2779
replies.body: Yeah a single full run depending how many symbols it looks at can take a while. For me with exploring 200 and selecting 20 for review takes about 15 minutes on my hardware. Again I cannot thank you enough for back testing!

replies.author: FantasticShine4012
replies.body: Here are your results.  April 2026 | 785 combinations tested | 7 asset classes | 670+ instruments Full-period backtests | 18 strategies | M1 to D1 timeframes ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── MARKET OVERVIEW ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Market | Avg PF | Win Rate | Expectancy | Strong Edges | Best Setup ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Indices | 1.574 | 54.2% | 0.168 | 40 | NAS100 / ORB (PF 3.49) US Stocks | 1.471 | 49.2% | 0.100 | 14 | NFLX / Volume Climax Reversal (PF 7.72) Crypto | 1.348 | 51.8% | 0.136 | 0 | BNB / Momentum Continuation (PF 1.70) ETF | 1.333 | 51.7% | 0.143 | 7 | EFA / Trend Continuation (PF 2.22) Forex | 1.127 | 50.0% | 0.054 | 0 | EURUSD / EMA Pullback (PF 1.60) Metals | 1.126 | 50.1% | 0.054 | 0 | XAGUSD / Breakout Retest (PF 1.44) Commodities | ERROR | — | — | — | Run failed, retest needed ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── DIRECT vs SLINGSHOT ENTRY ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Market | Direct PF | Direct WR | Slingshot PF | Slingshot WR | Winner ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Indices | 1.908 | 58.7% | 1.239 | 49.8% | DIRECT US Stocks | 1.537 | 50.1% | 1.402 | 48.2% | DIRECT Crypto | 1.269 | 50.8% | 1.427 | 52.8% | SLINGSHOT ETF | 1.268 | 53.1% | 1.399 | 50.4% | SLINGSHOT Forex | 1.038 | 49.5% | 1.216 | 50.5% | SLINGSHOT Metals | 0.987 | 47.9% | 1.264 | 52.3% | SLINGSHOT ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Note: Direct entry on Metals is loss-making (PF < 1.0) DATA COVERAGE ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Asset Class | Symbols | Timeframes | Period ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Forex | 20 | M1/M5/M15/M30/H1/H4/D1 | Jan 2012 - Apr 2026 US Stocks | 513 | M1, D1 | Jan 2018 - Dec 2023 ETFs | 117 | D1 | Sep 2003 - Apr 2026 Crypto Spot | 102 | M1/M5/M15/M30/H1/H4/D1 | Dec 2023 - Apr 2026 Commodities | 20 | M1/M5/M15/M30/H1/H4/D1 | Jan 2012 - Apr 2026 Indices | 19 | M1/M5/M15/M30/H1/H4/D1 | Jan 2024 - Apr 2026 Metals | 14 | M1/M5/M15/M30/H1/H4 | Dec 2025 - Apr 2026 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── KEY FINDINGS ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── 1. Indices is the strongest market — NAS100/ORB hits PF 3.49, 40 strong-edge combos 2. NFLX/Volume Climax Reversal produced the highest single PF of 7.72 3. Slingshot entry wins in 4 of 6 markets (Crypto, ETF, Forex, Metals) 4. Direct entry on Metals is loss-making — PF 0.987, never use without filter 5. Forex has zero strong edges — generic strategies need forex-specific refinement 6. Crypto shows consistent weak edge across all 180 combinations — zero failures METHODOLOGY ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── - ATR-based SL (1.5x ATR14) | TP1 at 1R, TP2 at 2R, TP3 at 3R - Time stop: forced close at 6/12/18 bars if TP1 not hit - Trailing stop to breakeven after TP1 - Strong Edge threshold: PF >= 1.5, Expectancy >= 0.3, WR >= 45% - Minimum 30 trades per combination for valid result - Full period backtests, no walk-forward - Total compute: 825 minutes on dedicated backtesting server ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

replies.author: No-Arm-2779
replies.body: Awesome thank you so much man! I'll see how I can improve my setup.

replies.author: FantasticShine4012
replies.body: No problem.  I am improving my marketdata system and will be online soon.  You just got a free test from me.

comments.author: Spirited_Syllabub488
comments.body:
Hey guys, I have also successfully built an profitable ML trading system for BTC and Gold that I genuinely trust. 5 years backtested, averaging around 10% per month on fixed lot, Sharpe above 3.

Been live trading it since September, and the model stayed positive through the entire spot drawdown. That part I'm most proud of honestly.

I don't have the capital to scale it myself so I'm looking for someone serious who would acquire it. Full backtest report here: [https://drive.google.com/file/d/1xwNisxVslkfPPY9g2rcbWt4shrI-9o4-/view?usp=drive\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1xwNisxVslkfPPY9g2rcbWt4shrI-9o4-/view?usp=drive_link)

If it looks interesting to someone, drop a comment or reach out, happy to have a straightforward conversation. Also will be providing the live trade data if anyone really interested in my profitable system.

comments.author: Ok-Sail9420
comments.body: let it run for 30 days and come back to us

comments.author: jciccare
comments.body: What did you build in? I also built one awhile ago but I continue to update I feel mine is also bad ass.

comments.author: Re-tr0_
comments.body: You said you ran this on local models. Are you running it on like a Mac mini? What model are you using?

replies.author: No-Arm-2779
replies.body: I'm running it off a rx68000 with 16gb of vram, the models I used were qwen3.5 4b for the argument models and qwen3.5 9b for the executor model

replies.author: Re-tr0_
replies.body: Oh wow that’s nice to know. I initially bought a Mac mini but only ran the Claude opus 4.6 model because every one said it was the better model industry wise. It was super expensive because the API cost is so much for opus and I eventually had to shut down my trading bot. I heard about qwen and how I can run it locally making it practically free besides electricity cost. I’m very interested in how you set this up.
